  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Section asserts a complexity bound matching a specific lower bound, invokes a Lyapunov proof sketch, and claims superiority over Allen-Zhu/Lan-Zhou in the non-uniform-smoothness regime; logical consistency of these chained technical claims is centrally at stake.",
    "R1.3": "Numerical experiment (logistic regression, Pareto L_i) is cited as direct evidence for the theoretical rate; alignment between that experimental description and the O(·) bound needs checking.",
    "R1.4": "An explicit 'first algorithm' novelty claim is load-bearing if the request is technical review; prior accelerated VR methods are listed, making delta assessment central.",
    "R1.6": "Bare 'help me with this section' is the canonical trigger for prose-level assistance; writing polish is therefore plausibly the primary intent.",
    "R1.7": "Multiple citations carry specific theorem pointers (Volkov and Petrenko 2018 Thm 3.2) and year/author details that require accuracy verification if scholarship review is intended."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Are you asking for (A) a technical review of the mathematical claims, complexity bounds, proof sketch, and novelty assertions, or (B) writing and presentation improvements to the prose?"
}

rationale.R1.2: Section asserts a complexity bound matching a specific lower bound, invokes a Lyapunov proof sketch, and claims superiority over Allen-Zhu/Lan-Zhou in the non-uniform-smoothness regime; logical consistency of these chained technical claims is centrally at stake.
rationale.R1.3: Numerical experiment (logistic regression, Pareto L_i) is cited as direct evidence for the theoretical rate; alignment between that experimental description and the O(·) bound needs checking.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit 'first algorithm' novelty claim is load-bearing if the request is technical review; prior accelerated VR methods are listed, making delta assessment central.
rationale.R1.6: Bare 'help me with this section' is the canonical trigger for prose-level assistance; writing polish is therefore plausibly the primary intent.
rationale.R1.7: Multiple citations carry specific theorem pointers (Volkov and Petrenko 2018 Thm 3.2) and year/author details that require accuracy verification if scholarship review is intended.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for (A) a technical review of the mathematical claims, complexity bounds, proof sketch, and novelty assertions, or (B) writing and presentation improvements to the prose?
